name: Virginia Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 9 x 13-inch baking pan; set aside.
2. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan, combine milk, butter, corn syrup, granulated sugar and brown sugar.
3. Place over medium heat and stir occasionally with a wooden spoon until the mixture comes to a boil.
4. Clip on a candy thermometer.
5. Cook to 234°F (115°C) or soft-ball stage.
6. Pour into baking pan without scraping.
7. Cool to lukewarm.
8. Add vanilla; stir with a wooden spoon until the mixture thickens.
9. Add nuts and continue stirring until the candy loses its gloss.
10. Scrape out onto plastic wrap.
11. Pat into a loaf shape (about 9 inches x 5 inches).
12. Slice and serve, or wrap in plastic and store in the refrigerator for several weeks.
Variation: Use 1 teaspoon maple extract instead of vanilla.
